channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Indian Activist on Hunger Strike Is Forcibly Removed by Police
item.description: The activist, Sonam Wangchuk, started his strike last month as part of a protest over educational mismanagement that has affected millions of students.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal

media:content.media:description: The authorities carrying the activist Sonam Wangchuk on a stretcher in New Delhi on Saturday.

item.title: Un meteorito cayó en una casa en Nueva Jersey. Es un tesoro para la ciencia
item.description: Una pareja de Nueva Jersey ayudó a los científicos a descubrir la composición de un asteroide inusual después de que cayera en su habitación.
item.dc:creator: Robin George Andrews

item.title: How the MC Hotel in Montclair, N.J., Became a World Cup Hot Spot
item.description: A boutique hotel in Montclair, N.J., has been home base for England, Norway, Germany and now Spain. It has also drawn crowds of fans hoping for a glimpse of soccer stars.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Maslin Nir

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A month into the World Cup, the soccer fans of Montclair know the routine for how to spot the players.

item.title: Trump and Mamdani to Attend World Cup Final on Sunday
item.description: They have no plans to meet, but it is the second time in a month that the two ubiquitous political figures will be at the same signature sporting event.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump and Mayor Zohran Mamdani, both New Yorkers, have maintained an unlikely relationship despite their political differences.

item.title: Haz ejercicio en la playa: más músculos, menos tiempo
item.description: Correr, hacer yoga o simplemente caminar descalzo en la orilla puede fortalecer el tren inferior, mejorar la estabilidad y reducir el estrés, siempre que se tomen las precauciones adecuadas.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Amanda Friedman para The New York Times

item.title: They Don’t Fight Crime, but They Do Eat Bugs: The Bats of N.Y.C.
item.description: Several species of bats have made the city’s patchwork green spaces their homes, much to the surprise of some scientists.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Rosenfield and Dave Sanders

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 18, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Lionel Messi camina hacia la historia
item.description: Messi no camina porque se haya ido del partido. Camina para entrar en él.
item.dc:creator: Rowan Ricardo Phillips

item.title: As Trump Scraps With Meloni, His Envoy to Italy Is at Sea
item.description: Tilman J. Fertitta, the U.S. ambassador to Rome, is summering on his superyacht as President Trump squabbles with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich and Gianni Cipriano

media:content.media:description: Tilman Fertitta aboard his yacht, Boardwalk, on the Fourth of July.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Picking Trash Out of Muck, and Loving It
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, July 18, 2026.

item.title: They Called Sam Neill ‘Skux.’ (It Was a Compliment.)
item.description: The New Zealand slang word, dating back to the 1990s, was pulled back into circulation in tributes to the actor, who died on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

media:content.media:description: Sam Neill at the Toronto International Film Festival in 2019.

item.title: Corrections: July 18, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, July 18, 2026.

item.title: Halloumi Souvlaki Bowl
item.description: With just a few building blocks — seared halloumi, sautéed chickpeas and yogurty rice— this recipe yields a quick and easy dinner in a bowl.

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Hadas Smirnoff.

item.title: Grilled Steak With Sauce Rof
item.description: A combination of onion, parsley, scallions and chile, this classic Senegalese sauce adds complexity to dishes.

media:content.media:credit: Armando Rafael for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Cyd Raftus McDowell.

item.title: Tahini Krispie Chocolate Bars
item.description: These sweet and salty bars are a low-effort, high-reward treat that upgrades your average Rice Krispies treat.

media:content.media:credit: Julia Gartland for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Rebecca Jurkevich.

item.title: Grabber
item.description: Ryan Judge’s latest themeless puzzle has a bite to it!
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: It’s a tough job, but somebody’s got to do it.

item.title: Rhode Island Man Who Fled 2005 Rape Trial Is Arrested in New York
item.description: Ronald L. Fischer vanished just before he was convicted. After more than 20 years on the run, he was arrested on a sailboat in the East River, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

item.title: How Trump Is Using Federal Agencies to Target Elections
item.description: Nick Corasaniti, a Politics reporter, explains how the Trump administration is deploying federal agencies to investigate and reshape electoral processes under state jurisdiction.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Corasaniti, Laura Bult, Nour Idriss, June Kim and Stephanie Swart

item.title: U.S. Forest Service Employees Are Freed After 15-Hour Hostage Ordeal
item.description: The authorities said a man and his son held two employees at gunpoint in a trailer at Shasta-Trinity National Forest in Northern California.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

item.title: First Patient Dies of Legionnaires’ Disease After New York Outbreak
item.description: New York City’s Health Department is still seeking to identify the source of an outbreak on the Upper East Side that has infected 67 people.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Mimi d’Autremont for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Twelve patients remained hospitalized with Legionnaires’ disease as of Thursday night.

item.title: 7 Americans Sent to Disputed Kenya Ebola Site After New Trump Travel Ban
item.description: The aid workers were on the frontline in the fight against the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and have shown no symptoms, their employer said.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli and Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: A satellite image showing the field hospital at Laikipia Air Base in central Kenya, where America aid workers are being quarantined.

item.title: Mourning One Tragedy at Sea, a Family Is Devastated by Another
item.description: The Boisas went out on the San Francisco Bay to spread the ashes of one of their own. Then a wave toppled their boat.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla, Madaleine Rubin and Heather Knight

media:content.media:credit: Stephen Lam/San Francisco Chronicle, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Alcatraz Island a day after a boat named Volare capsized in rough seas near the island.

item.title: AIPAC Closes Donor Portal to Democrats Who Voted to End Aid to Israel
item.description: The pro-Israel lobbying group appeared to be punishing Democrats it had endorsed for re-election after they voted in favor of an amendment that would have cut off U.S. aid to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In AIPAC’s web portal, the option of donating to House Democrats who voted to end U.S. aid to Israel was unavailable. That included Representative Katherine Clark of Massachusetts, left.

item.title: South Carolina Man Who Fired Rifle to Scare a Black Neighbor Gets 9 Years
item.description: The man, Jonathan Andrew Felkel, fired a rifle and shouted a racial slur at the victim, whom he targeted because of his race, law enforcement officials said.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

item.title: Trump Threatens Canada With Tariffs Over Wildfires
item.description: President Trump threatened on Friday to charge Canada tariffs for harms caused by the wildfire smoke in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: Dozens of Wildfires Are Suddenly Burning in Oregon
item.description: The state told thousands of people to be ready to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

item.title: Zohran Mamdani Knows He Has Political Capital. And He Intends to Spend It.
item.description: Lulu Garcia-Navarro speaks with Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York City about his affordability agenda.

item.title: Paul Pelosi Charged With Hit-and-Run in Napa Valley Crash
item.description: Mr. Pelosi, the 86-year-old husband of former Speaker Nancy Pelosi, crashed into a parked Tesla near his home among the vineyards this month.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka

media:content.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Paul Pelosi crashed his convertible into a parked Tesla and continued to drive down a street in Yountville, Calif., on July 3.

item.title: Central Texas Floods Kills at Least Two People
item.description: A deadly flood that tore through Central Texas for days began to ebb in some areas on Friday. At least two people died in the flooding. Gov. Greg Abbott warned residents that water levels were still expected to rise.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Jordan Vonderhaar for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The rainfall in some areas may have even surpassed what fell last year during deadly storms in the region.

item.title: Billionaires Prepare $87 Million Ad Campaign to Block California Wealth Tax
item.description: A coalition backed by the Google co-founder Sergey Brin and wealthy tech investors is trying to neutralize a proposed one-time tax on the state’s richest residents.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer and Laurel Rosenhall
item.category: Service Employees International Union-United Healthcare Workers West

media:content.media:description: A campaign event for a proposed billionaire tax in Los Angeles in February.

item.title: Trump Endorses Graham for Senate, Scrambling South Carolina Primary
item.description: Senator Darline Graham had been seen as a caretaker for her brother’s seat after his sudden death. Now she has the president’s nod in what was shaping up to be a crowded fight to succeed Lindsey Graham.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison

media:content.media:description: Darline Graham, left, was sworn in on Tuesday at the Capitol.

item.title: ‘Vámonos para La Guaira’: las voces de los rescatistas en Venezuela
item.description: ¿Quiénes son los voluntarios que trabajan en las operaciones después de los terremotos y por qué se movilizaron para ayudar? The New York Times habló con ellos.

item.title: How Is America Doing? New Yorkers Weigh In.
item.description: How is your America doing? With the U.S. midterm elections less than four months away, Times Opinion wanted to hear from voters. Mara Gay, a staff writer for Opinion, traveled to the Hudson Valley to ask people what’s on their minds.
item.dc:creator: Mara Gay and Emily Holzknecht

item.title: Bridges and Water Plants Attacked as Iran War Escalates
item.description: Also, U.S. start-up activity is booming. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: An image from social media shows bridges reduced to rubble in southern Iran.

item.title: In Venezuela, These Rescue Workers and Volunteers Rushed Into the Earthquake Zone
item.description: After the quakes, Venezuelans knew they had to fend for themselves. They explain why.
item.dc:creator: Isayen Herrera, Luis Ferré-Sadurní, Patricia Sulbarán and Fabiola Ferrero

media:content.media:credit: Fabiola Ferrero for The New York Times

item.title: Who Really Has the 2026 Midterms Cash Edge?
item.description: The fund-raising race is tighter than it seems.
item.dc:creator: Jess Bidgood

media:content.media:credit: Antranik Tavitian for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Supporters of Ken Paxton last month at the 2026 Republican Party of Texas convention in Houston. Mr. Paxton, the party’s nominee for Senate, has been drastically out-raised by his Democratic rival, James Talarico.

item.title: On Fire. Again.
item.description: There were orange skies in 2023, 2025 and now again in 2026. Over the last few years, one thing has become glaringly clear: The East Coast is totally unequipped for the next climate disaster, wildfires. The Opinion writer David Wallace-Wells explains that with every climate disaster, we’re not just adapting to a “new normal,” but bracing ourselves for what’s next.
item.dc:creator: David Wallace-Wells

item.title: Is It Safe to Eat Lettuce?
item.description: Federal health officials have linked a large cyclosporiasis outbreak to shredded lettuce served at Taco Bell. Is lettuce from grocery stores and restaurants OK?
item.dc:creator: Dani Blum and Roni Caryn Rabin

item.title: Brote de ‘Cyclospora’: Taylor Farms retirará algunos productos
item.description: La empresa de California cultiva productos agrícolas en más de 12 estados, así como en México y Canadá. Algunas de sus lechugas sospechosas de causar enfermedades terminaron en Taco Bell.
item.dc:creator: Julie Creswell, Christina Jewett and Kevin Draper
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

item.title: The Danger of Trump’s Election Obsession
item.description: “Trump rejects the idea that he can lose,” the Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie says. As the president weaponizes the federal government to chase election conspiracies, Bouie warns that Trump will do anything to avoid “narcissistic injury” — even if it means dismantling the right to free and fair elections.
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie and Ingrid Holmquist

item.title: Firms With Checkered Pasts Tied to Troubled Midtown Construction Project
item.description: Among those connected to companies that worked on 235 East 42nd Street were a man who once bribed city officials and a developer who gave a loan to Bernard Kerik.
item.dc:creator: Dionne Searcey, William K. Rashbaum, Stefanos Chen and Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:description: Two columns inside a Midtown high-rise construction project buckled last week, sowing panic and causing evacuations around the East 42nd Street site.

item.title: Trump Pursues a Deeper Bond With China’s Leader, Despite Hostile Speech
item.description: Chinese officials appear to think that President Trump’s accusations that China interfered in the 2020 U.S. elections were driven by domestic politics, not foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong

media:content.media:description: Xi Jinping and President Trump participated in events at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing in May.

item.title: Ron Hunt, a Record-Holder for Being Hit by Pitches, Dies at 85
item.description: Though he was notable as a Met, his record for being hit by pitches the most times in a modern-era season — 50 — came while playing for the Expos.
item.dc:creator: Richard Goldstein

media:content.media:description: Ron Hunt in 1962, when he was acquired by the Mets. The next year, he was the runner-up in balloting to be the National League’s rookie of the year.

item.title: How Trump Abused the Power to Declassify America’s Secrets
item.description: The president is manipulating the intelligence community into a tool of misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Massimo Calabresi

media:content.media:credit: Joseph Rushmore for The New York Times

item.title: China Pushes for ‘Openness’ to Shape A.I.’s Future
item.description: At an artificial intelligence conference in Shanghai on Friday, Xi Jinping urged for open and global collaboration, aimed at supporting the technological advancement of developing countries.
item.dc:creator: David Cheung

item.title: Sara Rodriguez, Lieutenant Governor, Quits Wisconsin Governor’s Race
item.description: One establishment candidate dropped out and another rejoined the race amid worries from some moderate Democrats that a democratic socialist might win the primary.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman and Mitch Smith

media:content.media:credit: Jenn Ackerman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez’s withdrawal upended the race in Wisconsin.

item.title: Trump’s Election Claims and SAVE Act Push Find Muted Response From G.O.P. Lawmakers
item.description: Most G.O.P. lawmakers had little to say about the president’s claims of election vulnerabilities, and he did not appear to move the needle on the voting restriction bill he championed.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold, Carl Hulse, Reid J. Epstein and Erik Wemple

media:content.media:description: President Trump, seen on a screen during his speech at the White House on Thursday. Mr. Trump has made baseless assertions of election fraud for years, largely pointing to a similar set of concerns.

item.title: Un artista y disidente cubano se irá al exilio tras cumplir su condena
item.description: Otero Alcántara fue arrestado durante las protestas antigubernamentales masivas de 2021 y permaneció cinco años en prisión. EE. UU. ofreció una visa y se espera que llegue a Miami el sábado.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara en La Habana en 2021

item.title: The Rise and Fall of Ukraine’s Drone Warfare Mastermind
item.description: The popularity of drones, and the defense minister who heavily promoted them, helped lead to a clash in which President Volodymyr Zelensky sided with the military brass.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer

media:content.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mykhailo Fedorov, the former minister of defense for Ukraine, speaking Thursday in Kyiv. Mr. Fedorov’s growing reliance on drones had reportedly irked some members of the military’s leadership.

item.title: Karim Khan’s Accuser Breaks Silence on Alleged Sexual Misconduct at I.C.C.
item.description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor at the International Criminal Court, has denied the accusations. He faces a vote next week that will determine whether he keeps his job.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub and Jeanna Smialek

media:content.media:description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court, at a U.N. Security Council meeting.
